back_translation: Welcome the one who is weak in faith — not for the purpose of judging his opinions
One person believes that he can eat all things — but the one who is weak eats only vegetables
The one who eats, let him not despise the one who does not eat — and the one who does not eat, let him not judge the one who eats — for God (royally) has welcomed him
Who are you to judge another's servant? Whether he stands or falls depends on his own master — but he will stand, for the Lord (royally) is able to make him stand
One person regards one day above another — another regards every day alike. Let each one be fully convinced in his own mind

The one who observes a day, observes it for the Lord — the one who eats, eats for the Lord, for he gives thanks to God — and the one who does not eat, does not eat for the Lord, and also gives thanks to God
For none of us lives for himself — and none of us dies for himself
For if we live, we live for the Lord — and if we die, we die for the Lord. Therefore, whether we live or whether we die, we belong to the Lord
For this reason Christ (royally) died and (royally) lives — so that He may (royally) be the Lord of both the dead and the living
But why do you judge your brother? Or why do you despise your brother? For we will all stand before the judgment seat of God
For it is written: 'The Lord (royally) says — as surely as I live, every knee will bow before Me, and every tongue will confess to God'
Therefore, each of us will give an account concerning himself
Therefore, let us not judge one another any longer — but rather judge this — namely, not to put a stumbling block or obstacle before your brother
I know and am persuaded in the Lord Jesus that nothing is unclean by itself — except for the one who reckons something to be unclean — for that one, it is unclean
For if your brother is grieved because of food, you are no longer walking according to love. Do not destroy by your food the one for whom Christ (royally) died
Therefore, do not let your good thing be spoken of in an evil sense
For the kingdom of God is not a matter of food and drink — but righteousness, peace, and joy in the Holy (royal) Spirit
For the one who serves Christ in this way is pleasing to God and approved by humans
Therefore, let us pursue the things of peace and the things of mutual edification
Do not destroy the work of God for the sake of food. All things are clean — but it is evil for a person who eats with a stumbling block
It is good not to eat meat or drink wine — or to do anything by which your brother stumbles, is obstructed, or is weakened
The faith you have, keep between yourself and God. The one who does not condemn himself in what he approves is blessed
But the one who hesitates, if he eats, is condemned — because he does not eat from faith — and everything that does not come from faith is sin
